constraints: Sum of |S| over all test cases <= 10^6
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a string S representing a candidate password. The string consists only of lowercase letters, uppercase letters, digits, and the special characters '@', '#', '%', '&', '?'.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if the password satisfies all security conditions, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Case 1: The only digit '1' is at the last position, so digit is not strictly inside. Case 2: All required characters appear inside and length >=10. Case 3: Length is 8 (<10), so condition fails.
Length is 10, contains lowercase, uppercase 'B' at position 1, digit '1' at position 2, special '@' at position 3 – all inside requirements satisfied.
No digit, no special character, and the only uppercase 'A' is at the first position, so inside‑uppercase condition fails.

solution.approach: For each password we scan the characters once and maintain five boolean flags: hasLower (anywhere), hasUpperInside (uppercase not at first or last position), hasDigitInside, hasSpecialInside, and lengthOk (|S| >= 10). While scanning we update the flags according to the character and its index. After the scan we output YES if all flags are true, otherwise NO. This runs in O(|S|) per test case and O(1) extra memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(total length of all strings) which is ≤ 10^6
